name: HIPAA Security Rule
disclaimer: This provides technical guidance for implementing HIPAA compliance controls. It is NOT legal advice and does not constitute HIPAA certification. Consult qualified legal counsel and consider engaging a certified HIPAA auditor for formal compliance verification.

requirements.name: Security Management Process
requirements.description: Policies to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations

requirements.name: Risk Analysis
requirements.description: Accurate and thorough assessment of potential risks to ePHI

requirements.name: Information System Activity Review
requirements.description: Regular review of audit logs, access reports, and security incident tracking

requirements.name: Assigned Security Responsibility
requirements.description: Designate a HIPAA Security Officer

requirements.name: Termination Procedures
requirements.description: Revoke access when employment ends

requirements.name: Information Access Management
requirements.description: Authorize access consistent with Privacy Rule

requirements.name: Security Awareness and Training
requirements.description: Security awareness training program

requirements.name: Security Incident Procedures
requirements.description: Identify and respond to security incidents

requirements.name: Contingency Plan
requirements.description: Policies for responding to emergencies

requirements.name: Business Associate Contracts
requirements.description: Satisfactory assurances from business associates

requirements.name: Facility Access Controls
requirements.description: Limit physical access to electronic information systems

requirements.name: Workstation Use
requirements.description: Specify proper functions and physical attributes of workstations

requirements.name: Workstation Security
requirements.description: Physical safeguards restricting access to authorized users

requirements.name: Access Control
requirements.description: Technical policies to allow access only to authorized persons

requirements.name: Automatic Logoff
requirements.description: Terminate session after period of inactivity

requirements.name: Encryption and Decryption
requirements.description: Encrypt and decrypt ePHI at rest

requirements.name: Audit Controls
requirements.description: Record and examine system activity

requirements.name: Integrity
requirements.description: Protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction

requirements.name: Person or Entity Authentication
requirements.description: Verify identity of person seeking access

requirements.name: Transmission Security
requirements.description: Guard against unauthorized access during transmission

requirements.name: Business Associate Contracts
requirements.description: Document satisfactory assurances from business associates

requirements.name: Policies and Procedures
requirements.description: Implement reasonable and appropriate policies

requirements.name: Documentation
requirements.description: Maintain written policies and records of required actions

requirements.name: Uses and Disclosures
requirements.description: Permitted uses and disclosures of PHI

requirements.name: Safe Harbor De-identification
requirements.description: 18 HIPAA Safe Harbor identifiers

checklist.text: Security Management Process — policies to prevent, detect, contain, and correct security violations
Risk Analysis — accurate and thorough assessment of potential risks to ePHI
Risk Management — security measures to reduce risks to reasonable level
Sanction Policy — sanctions against workforce members who violate policies
Information System Activity Review — regular review of audit logs and access reports
Assigned Security Responsibility — designate a HIPAA Security Officer
Workforce Security — ensure appropriate access and prevent unauthorized access
Authorization and Supervision — authorize and supervise workforce ePHI access
Workforce Clearance Procedure — determine appropriate access levels
Termination Procedures — revoke access when employment ends
Information Access Management — authorize access consistent with Privacy Rule
Access Authorization — policies for granting ePHI access
Access Establishment and Modification — procedures for creating and revoking access
Security Awareness and Training — security awareness training program
Security Reminders — periodic security updates to workforce
Protection from Malicious Software — procedures for guarding against malware
Log-in Monitoring — procedures for monitoring log-in attempts
Password Management — procedures for creating, changing, safeguarding passwords
Security Incident Procedures — identify and respond to security incidents
Response and Reporting — mitigate harmful effects and document incidents
Contingency Plan — policies for responding to emergencies
Data Backup Plan — create and maintain retrievable copies of ePHI
Disaster Recovery Plan — procedures to restore lost data
Emergency Mode Operation Plan — critical business processes during emergency
Testing and Revision — periodically test and revise contingency plans
Evaluation — periodic technical and non-technical evaluation
Business Associate Contracts — satisfactory assurances from business associates
Facility Access Controls — limit physical access to electronic information systems
Facility Security Plan — safeguard facility and equipment
Workstation Use — specify proper functions and physical attributes of workstations
Workstation Security — physical safeguards restricting access to authorized users
Device and Media Controls — govern receipt and removal of hardware and media
Disposal — procedures for final disposal of ePHI and hardware
Access Control — technical policies to allow access only to authorized persons
Unique User Identification — assign unique name/number to track user identity
Emergency Access Procedure — procedures for obtaining ePHI during emergency
Automatic Logoff — terminate session after period of inactivity
Encryption and Decryption — encrypt and decrypt ePHI at rest
Audit Controls — record and examine system activity
Integrity — protect ePHI from improper alteration or destruction
Mechanism to Authenticate ePHI — corroborate that ePHI has not been altered
Person or Entity Authentication — verify identity of person seeking access
Transmission Security — guard against unauthorized access during transmission
Integrity Controls — ensure transmitted ePHI is not improperly modified
Encryption — encrypt ePHI in transit
Business Associate Contracts — document satisfactory assurances from BAs
BA Contract Requirements — establish permitted uses and disclosures
Policies and Procedures — implement reasonable and appropriate policies
Documentation — maintain written policies and records of required actions
Time Limit — retain documentation for 6 years
